title: QUIC RFC 9001 Large Key-Update Epoch Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home execution, adjacent repeated key-update regression tests, solution build, SpecTrace JSON validation, Workbench core validation, and git diff hygiene.
scope: Verify that repeated 1-RTT key-update epoch identifiers are no longer capped at the old 32-bit implementation boundary, while the packet Key Phase signal remains derived from the epoch low bit and retained-old cleanup keeps matching the retained wide epoch.
preconditions: REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0006 remains the repeated epoch ownership baseline.
The runtime already owns repeated local and peer install gates, current-phase acknowledgment, cooldown state, retained-old material, and sender/recovery cleanup synchronization.
procedure: Run the focused requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0007.
Rerun adjacent REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0006 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003 regression tests.
Run `dotnet tool run workbench -- --format json validate --profile core`.
Run `git diff --check` before committing the slice.
expected_result: The bridge installs repeated local and peer 1-RTT key updates from epoch uint.MaxValue to uint.MaxValue + 1, the packet Key Phase bit wraps to zero, retained-old state records the previous wide epoch, and lifecycle cleanup state preserves current acknowledgment and cooldown ownership without narrowing the epoch.
status_summary: Passed locally on 2026-04-30. `dotnet build Incursa.Quic.slnx -c Release` completed with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Focused execution for `REQ_QUIC_RFC9001_S6P5_0007|REQ_QUIC_RFC9001_S6P5_0006|REQ_QUIC_RFC9001_S6P5_0003` passed 18/18. `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts\Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 -Profiles core` validated 313 SpecTrace JSON artifacts. `dotnet tool run workbench -- --format json validate --profile core` returned ok with 0 errors and 0 warnings. `dotnet test Incursa.Quic.slnx -c Release --no-build -m:1` passed 3,299/3,299. Broader unbounded key-update lifecycle support remains out of scope.
